sections.paragraph:
νηπιάζω (νήπιος; Hippocr. IX 360 L.; Erinna Lyrica [IV b.c.]: PSI IX, 1090, 55+15 [p.xii]=AnthLG, Erinna Fgm. 1 B, 29 Diehl; Memnon Hist. [I a.d.], Fgm. 22, 1 CMüller; Porphyr., πρὸς Γαῦρον [ABA 1895] 12, 3 p. 50, 27; 12, 4 p. 51, 6; schol. on Eur., Phoen. 713) be (as) a child fig., w. dat. (Hippocr., Ep. 17, 25 ἐπιβουλῇσι νηπιάζειν) τῇ κακίᾳ in evil 1 Cor 14:20.—DELG s.v. νήπιος. M-M. TW.

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

  1. L. = KLachmann, editor of the Greek NT (1842–50)—List 1, beg.

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

AnthLG AnthLG = Anthologia Lyrica Graeca—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

Memnon Memnon (of Heracleon?), I b.c.–I? a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

dat. dat. = dative

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 671
